column1: Provide safe, decent, and affordable housing for income-eligible residents of Sumter County. The Division encourages homeownership, helps to enhance residential neighborhoods, and assists with creating diverse, affordable housing opportunities. Housing assistance can improve quality of life, strengthen families, and promote economic growth. Access to quality affordable housing is a crucial aspect of maintaining a sustainable community.

It is the policy of Sumter County, in keeping with the laws of the United States of America and the spirit of the Constitution of the State of Florida, to promote through fair, orderly and lawful procedure the opportunity for each person so desiring to obtain housing of such person's choice in this county, without regard to race, color, ancestry, pregnancy, national origin, religion, sex, marital status, familial status, handicap, or age, and, to that end, to prohibit discrimination in housing by any person. For more information on Fair Housing contact Denna Lafferty, Housing Coordinator.

Multifamily Housing assists rural property owners through loans, loan guarantees, and grants to develop and rehabilitate properties for low-income, elderly, domestic farm laborers, disabled individuals, and their families.

Multifamily Housing subsidizes rents for eligible low-income tenants who cannot afford to pay their full rent by providing vouchers to the owners of its direct and farm labor housing loan properties. When a direct loan ends before its initial term, Multifamily Housing can protect eligible tenants who otherwise may face hardship if they cannot find other affordable housing. [...] ## Quick Links

Rural Rental Housing and Farm Labor Housing projects must be established on a nonprofit or limited profit basis

column3: midfloridanewspapers.com,"Kudos to Lake County leaders and Leesburg City Commissioners who recently found a solution by working with Dominium, a national leader in attainable housing that has been working in Florida for over a decade.

column1: Lake County approved funding and the City of Leesburg approved a PUD change paving the way for a beautiful, amenity-filled complex with 276 residential rentals for low and moderate-income families. The Dominium project, with generous buffers on 18 acres near Lake Denham, will include a pool, clubhouse and community center. The two-, three- and four-bedroom apartments will be rent-restricted for families earning no more than 60% of the area’s median income. [...] In recent years, owning a home or finding affordable rentals has become nearly impossible for many working families, including here in Lake County. Housing and rental prices soared as construction costs and demand rose.
Communities faced an alarming problem: Many working families found they earned too much to qualify for subsidized housing but could not afford market-rate homes.

Even modest homes and apartments became out of reach for essential workers, including many teachers, police officers, nurses and firefighters. It’s a problem that has confounded city, county and state officials. [...] Dominium’s reputation goes well beyond building quality, attainable housing. They make scholarships available for residents and their dependents. They offer $100 Kindness Cards when residents have extraordinary hardships. Dominium employees are rewarded for volunteering in the community. The company offers residents opportunities for internships in diverse vocations, including accounting, marketing and property management.

Dominium is planning to take their mission of quality, workforce housing soon to Groveland and Minneola. That’s good news for South Lake. Attainable housing means a stable workforce and a higher quality of life for all.

Angie Langley is CEO of Langley Consulting Group in Clermont and helps businesses work with governments for land development throughout Florida.",serpapi;tavily,Lake County FL affordable housing,Leesburg,Lake County,unknown,search_result
